# PRIME v2 — Phase 2 Approval Package

**Prepared:** 2026-06-30
**Prepared by:** Ahron Jan Rafael (OJT intern)
**Phase:** 2 — MVP, Roles, and User Stories
**Status:** Documents complete — pending three approvals

---

## What Phase 2 Produced

Four documents were created from the information in README.md. They formalize exactly
what PRIME v2 will build, who can do what, and how proposals move through the system.

| Document | File | Approver Needed |
|---|---|---|
| MVP Specification | `docs/requirements/PRIME-v2-MVP.md` | **Product Owner** |
| Roles and Permissions | `docs/requirements/PRIME-v2-Roles-and-Permissions.md` | **Security Owner** |
| Workflow and Statuses | `docs/workflows/PRIME-v2-Workflow.md` | **Process Owner** |
| User Story Backlog | `docs/requirements/USER-STORY-BACKLOG.md` | **Product Owner** (with MVP) |

---

## Summary of Each Document

### MVP Specification (`PRIME-v2-MVP.md`)

Defines the 20-step end-to-end scenario that must work for the MVP to be considered complete.
Contains a 70+ item pass/fail checklist across 14 categories:
authentication, proposal creation, versioning, comments, focal workflow,
RTEC review, budget/accounting/RD, notifications, audit log, attachments,
PDF export, admin dashboard, status dashboard, and infrastructure.

Also includes: Definition of Ready, Definition of Done, success metrics,
and a 14-item out-of-scope table.

**Product Owner should confirm:**
- [ ] The 20-step MVP end-to-end scenario is correct
- [ ] Nothing important is missing from the checklist
- [ ] The out-of-scope table correctly excludes what should not be built
- [ ] The Definition of Done is strict enough

---

### Roles and Permissions (`PRIME-v2-Roles-and-Permissions.md`)

Defines 8 user roles, authentication rules (Google-only for applicants,
email/password for staff), and a detailed permission matrix covering:
- Proposal actions (create, view, submit, upload, export)
- Workflow transitions (who can endorse, return, approve at each stage)
- Comment actions (who can add, view, and resolve each comment type)
- Admin-only account management actions

Also defines 6 comment visibility types and 8 security constraints
that every backend API route must enforce.

**Security Owner should confirm:**
- [ ] The permission matrix correctly reflects organizational policy
- [ ] The comment visibility rules are correct (especially RTEC private comments)
- [ ] The 8 security constraints are sufficient
- [ ] Multi-role handling (a user with two roles) is correctly described
- [ ] No role has more access than it should

---

### Workflow and Statuses (`PRIME-v2-Workflow.md`)

Defines all 24 proposal statuses with plain-English definitions.
Includes a Mermaid diagram showing every allowed transition,
a full transition table (from → to → actor → conditions),
and the required audit fields for every status change.

**Process Owner should confirm:**
- [ ] All 24 statuses are correct and none are missing
- [ ] Every transition arrow in the diagram matches actual policy
- [ ] The return paths (Budget → Focal, Accounting → Budget or Focal) are correct
- [ ] The RTEC Head can return a member review for clarification (confirmed Stage 6)
- [ ] The Focal cannot reject outright — only return or endorse (confirmed Stage 3)
- [ ] A rejected proposal cannot be reopened — new proposal required (confirmed Stage 10)

---

## How to Approve

For each document:

1. Open the file
2. Review the relevant section above
3. If acceptable, fill in the Approval section at the top of the document:

```
| Approver | Role | Approval Date | Status |
| [Your name] | [Your role] | YYYY-MM-DD | Approved |
```

4. If changes are needed, note them and the intern will update and resubmit.

---

## What Happens After Approval

Once all three approvals are recorded:

- Phase 2 gate closes
- Phase 3 begins: analyzing and specifying each of the 27 proposal forms
- No coding starts until Phase 4 (Architecture) is also approved

The three approvals are independent — they can be done in any order or simultaneously.
